source_intake_record.raw_question_text: The elasticity of a rubber band can be described in terms of a onedimensional model of polymer involving $N$ molecules linked together end-to-end. The angle between successive links is equally likely to be $0^{\circ}$ or $180^{\circ}$. For $m \ll N$, this expression becomes $g(N, m) \approx g(N, 0) \exp \left(-2 m^{2} / N\right)$. Find the entropy of the system as a function of $L$ for $N \gg 1, L \ll N d$.

multi_step: solving requires converting the state-count expression into entropy via S = k ln g
the question asks for entropy as a function of L, so the solver must relate the model variable m to polymer length L
the final expression requires algebraic substitution and simplification under the stated limits N >> 1 and L << Nd
verifiability: answer_text is a short closed-form symbolic expression
the derivation is standard and can be checked from the provided approximation g(N,m) ≈ g(N,0) exp(-2m^2/N) plus the L-m relation

candidate_problem_record.raw_question_text: The elasticity of a rubber band can be described in terms of a onedimensional model of polymer involving $N$ molecules linked together end-to-end. The angle between successive links is equally likely to be $0^{\circ}$ or $180^{\circ}$. For $m \ll N$, this expression becomes $g(N, m) \approx g(N, 0) \exp \left(-2 m^{2} / N\right)$. Find the entropy of the system as a function of $L$ for $N \gg 1, L \ll N d$.

metadata.reasoning_chain: When $m \ll N, g(N, m) \approx g(N, 0) \exp \left(-2 m^{2} / N\right)$, the entropy of the system becomes $S=k \ln g(N, m)=k \ln g(N, 0)-\frac{k L^{2}}{2 N d^{2}}$.
